plan.title: Eliminate every remaining audited PRISM performance bottleneck by removing full-state refresh and hydration work from hot paths, suffixizing remaining persistence and reload flows, tightening locking boundaries, and validating that no audited hotspot remains on mutate, request, refresh, reload, startup, or background paths.

node.title: Milestone 1: Replace full refresh reconstruction with a persistent runtime and narrower lock boundaries

acceptance.label: Ordinary filesystem refreshes no longer construct a fresh WorkspaceIndexer or rehydrate graph, history, outcomes, coordination, and projections from scratch
Read-side refresh does not hold one shared per-root sync lock across long-running reload and rebuild work
No-op and small-delta compact.refreshWorkspace runs stay on an incremental runtime path with measurable latency reduction

plan.title: Eliminate every remaining audited PRISM performance bottleneck by removing full-state refresh and hydration work from hot paths, suffixizing remaining persistence and reload flows, tightening locking boundaries, and validating that no audited hotspot remains on mutate, request, refresh, reload, startup, or background paths.
plan.goal: Eliminate every remaining audited PRISM performance bottleneck by removing full-state refresh and hydration work from hot paths, suffixizing remaining persistence and reload flows, tightening locking boundaries, and validating that no audited hotspot remains on mutate, request, refresh, reload, startup, or background paths.

node.title: Milestone 2: Make repo memory ingestion and episodic materialization strictly suffix-based

acceptance.label: snapshot_revisions and load_episodic_snapshot stop reparsing the full repo memory JSONL and stop replaying the full sqlite memory_event_log on unchanged or append-only paths
Episodic save and reanchor paths use materialized state plus high-water marks instead of full event-log reconstruction
Refresh and reload ingest only appended memory events while preserving current semantics across local and shared runtime stores

plan.title: Eliminate every remaining audited PRISM performance bottleneck by removing full-state refresh and hydration work from hot paths, suffixizing remaining persistence and reload flows, tightening locking boundaries, and validating that no audited hotspot remains on mutate, request, refresh, reload, startup, or background paths.
plan.goal: Eliminate every remaining audited PRISM performance bottleneck by removing full-state refresh and hydration work from hot paths, suffixizing remaining persistence and reload flows, tightening locking boundaries, and validating that no audited hotspot remains on mutate, request, refresh, reload, startup, or background paths.

node.title: Milestone 3: Convert coordination reload and persistence to incremental read/write models

acceptance.label: Coordination read refresh no longer rebuilds snapshot, published plan projection, and live graphs from full event and log replay on each revision bump
Coordination mutate persistence appends events without global existing-event scans and without reloading the full published plan projection
Published plan synchronization only touches changed plans, changed logs, and necessary index entries while preserving hydration correctness

plan.title: Eliminate every remaining audited PRISM performance bottleneck by removing full-state refresh and hydration work from hot paths, suffixizing remaining persistence and reload flows, tightening locking boundaries, and validating that no audited hotspot remains on mutate, request, refresh, reload, startup, or background paths.
plan.goal: Eliminate every remaining audited PRISM performance bottleneck by removing full-state refresh and hydration work from hot paths, suffixizing remaining persistence and reload flows, tightening locking boundaries, and validating that no audited hotspot remains on mutate, request, refresh, reload, startup, or background paths.

node.title: Milestone 4: Batch and incrementalize projection concept maintenance

acceptance.label: Indexing and outcome application no longer trigger full rebuild_concepts work on every lineage batch or every single outcome event
Startup and reload projection overlay paths avoid duplicate whole-concept rebuild passes when combining local, shared, and repo knowledge
Projection maintenance recomputes only the concept state affected by changed lineage, outcomes, or curated concept inputs

plan.title: Eliminate every remaining audited PRISM performance bottleneck by removing full-state refresh and hydration work from hot paths, suffixizing remaining persistence and reload flows, tightening locking boundaries, and validating that no audited hotspot remains on mutate, request, refresh, reload, startup, or background paths.

node.title: Milestone 5: Burn down residual bottlenecks and prove the audit is closed

acceptance.label: Before/after benchmarks show that each audited hotspot has been removed or demoted to an explicitly documented cold or fallback path
Regression coverage locks in incremental refresh, reload, mutate, startup, and background throughput invariants introduced by the earlier milestones
A final audit confirms no remaining bottleneck from the original list is still present on a hot, request, mutate, reload, startup, or background path

node.title: Milestone 0: Establish performance baselines, budgets, and regression guardrails
node.summary: Verifying milestone-0 acceptance against live repo state and filling any remaining baseline, validation, and instrumentation gaps before unblocking Milestone 1.

acceptance.label: Benchmarks and traces cover no-op refresh, scoped refresh, stale auxiliary read refresh, coordination reload, coordination mutate, startup, and background indexing throughput
Every later milestone has explicit before/after metrics and a validation recipe attached before implementation starts
Instrumentation exposes lock hold time, replay volume, loaded bytes, and full-rebuild counts for the audited paths

node.title: Milestone 1: Replace full refresh reconstruction with a persistent runtime and narrower lock boundaries
node.summary: Measure request-path refresh behavior before and after the persistent runtime change using refresh traces and runtime telemetry, with emphasis on lockHoldMs, loadedBytes, replayVolume, fullRebuildCount, and no-op/small-delta refresh latency.

acceptance.label: Ordinary filesystem refreshes no longer construct a fresh WorkspaceIndexer or rehydrate graph, history, outcomes, coordination, and projections from scratch
Read-side refresh does not hold one shared per-root sync lock across long-running reload and rebuild work
No-op and small-delta compact.refreshWorkspace runs stay on an incremental runtime path with measurable latency reduction

node.title: Milestone 2: Make repo memory ingestion and episodic materialization strictly suffix-based
node.summary: Measure episodic and repo-memory reload work before and after suffix-based ingestion, using loadedBytes and replayVolume to confirm append-only updates stop scaling with total memory history.

acceptance.label: snapshot_revisions and load_episodic_snapshot stop reparsing the full repo memory JSONL and stop replaying the full sqlite memory_event_log on unchanged or append-only paths
Episodic save and reanchor paths use materialized state plus high-water marks instead of full event-log reconstruction
Refresh and reload ingest only appended memory events while preserving current semantics across local and shared runtime stores

node.title: Milestone 3: Convert coordination reload and persistence to incremental read/write models
node.summary: Measure coordination reload and mutation persistence before and after the incremental coordination path, using refresh traces plus loadedBytes, replayVolume, and fullRebuildCount to detect broad rehydration.

acceptance.label: Coordination read refresh no longer rebuilds snapshot, published plan projection, and live graphs from full event and log replay on each revision bump
Coordination mutate persistence appends events without global existing-event scans and without reloading the full published plan projection
Published plan synchronization only touches changed plans, changed logs, and necessary index entries while preserving hydration correctness

node.title: Milestone 4: Batch and incrementalize projection concept maintenance
node.summary: Measure projection and concept maintenance before and after batching/incrementalization so loadedBytes, replayVolume, and fullRebuildCount drop on lineage and outcome-driven refreshes.

acceptance.label: Indexing and outcome application no longer trigger full rebuild_concepts work on every lineage batch or every single outcome event
Startup and reload projection overlay paths avoid duplicate whole-concept rebuild passes when combining local, shared, and repo knowledge
Projection maintenance recomputes only the concept state affected by changed lineage, outcomes, or curated concept inputs

node.title: Milestone 5: Burn down residual bottlenecks and prove the audit is closed
node.summary: Use the final startup, refresh, mutate, and background measurements plus the refresh telemetry counters to verify the original audit is closed and no hot path still forces broad rebuild work.

acceptance.label: Before/after benchmarks show that each audited hotspot has been removed or demoted to an explicitly documented cold or fallback path
Regression coverage locks in incremental refresh, reload, mutate, startup, and background throughput invariants introduced by the earlier milestones
A final audit confirms no remaining bottleneck from the original list is still present on a hot, request, mutate, reload, startup, or background path

node.title: Milestone 0: Establish performance baselines, budgets, and regression guardrails
node.summary: Established milestone baselines and guardrails: attached per-milestone validation recipes and before/after measurement targets, exposed lockHoldMs plus loadedBytes/replayVolume/fullRebuildCount in refresh telemetry, and validated the refresh-path baseline with targeted prism-mcp tests.

acceptance.label: Benchmarks and traces cover no-op refresh, scoped refresh, stale auxiliary read refresh, coordination reload, coordination mutate, startup, and background indexing throughput
Every later milestone has explicit before/after metrics and a validation recipe attached before implementation starts
Instrumentation exposes lock hold time, replay volume, loaded bytes, and full-rebuild counts for the audited paths

node.title: Milestone 1: Replace full refresh reconstruction with a persistent runtime and narrower lock boundaries
node.summary: Active Milestone 1 work: read-side refresh now stays on the persistent runtime path for no-op and deferred cases, refresh telemetry exposes loadedBytes/replayVolume/fullRebuildCount, and remaining work is to reduce or remove reconstructive full-refresh indexing on ordinary filesystem refreshes.

acceptance.label: Ordinary filesystem refreshes no longer construct a fresh WorkspaceIndexer or rehydrate graph, history, outcomes, coordination, and projections from scratch
Read-side refresh does not hold one shared per-root sync lock across long-running reload and rebuild work
No-op and small-delta compact.refreshWorkspace runs stay on an incremental runtime path with measurable latency reduction

node.title: Milestone 1: Replace full refresh reconstruction with a persistent runtime and narrower lock boundaries
node.summary: FS refresh now rebuilds the transient indexer from the live Prism runtime instead of reconstructing graph/history/outcomes/coordination/projections from persisted snapshots; the replacement Prism preserves coordination context and session-local projection state, with refresh-focused regression coverage in prism-core.

acceptance.label: Ordinary filesystem refreshes no longer construct a fresh WorkspaceIndexer or rehydrate graph, history, outcomes, coordination, and projections from scratch
Read-side refresh does not hold one shared per-root sync lock across long-running reload and rebuild work
No-op and small-delta compact.refreshWorkspace runs stay on an incremental runtime path with measurable latency reduction
